title: Oddkit gauntlet pass on AMS PoC plan; SPEC.md is the new contract surface
content: Ran orient → preflight → challenge → gate against the AMS PoC plan as it stood after seven commits. Orient placed work in planning mode with partial confidence and surfaced three open questions: locked vs open decisions, irreversibility, and evidence-for-approach. Preflight pulled the dream-house writing and the aquifer-mcp principles ledger as start-here docs; named DoD as change description / verification / observed behavior / evidence / self-audit. Challenge surfaced the substantive gaps: no evidence cited, no alternatives named, no retraction conditions, reversibility not addressed, no risks acknowledged, no disconfirmer named, success criteria buried in POC-PLAN.md not surfaced as a contract. Gate returned NOT_READY with two unmet prerequisites: problem statement not consolidated, constraints not consolidated. The fix landed as SPEC.md at the top of the repo: a single locking surface that names problem, goal, falsifiable acceptance criteria, in-scope and out-of-scope, alternatives considered, risks with mitigations, one-way vs two-way doors, disconfirmers, open decisions still inside v1, and the post-PoC roadmap. Deeper docs (PROTOCOL.md, POC-INFRA.md, POC-PLAN.md, AMS.md) stay as the reference layer; SPEC.md is the contract. README updated to point to SPEC.md first.

title: When SPEC.md and any deeper doc disagree, SPEC.md wins until the next revision
content: The repo now has a documented revision discipline: SPEC.md is the lock, the deeper docs are reference, and disagreement between them is resolved in SPEC.md's favor until the deeper doc is brought back into alignment. Every load-bearing change updates SPEC.md first. The revision log at the bottom of SPEC.md is the audit trail.

title: Spreading the contract across many docs is itself a gap a gauntlet will surface
content: Six docs of design (AMS.md, PROTOCOL.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, POC-INFRA.md, POC-PLAN.md, PATTERNS.md, GLOSSARY.md, plus journal) had every necessary piece but no single locking surface. The oddkit_gate tool returned NOT_READY not because the problem statement was absent but because it was distributed. The discipline lesson: depth and consolidation are different work products. Depth lives in many docs; consolidation lives in one. A spec/PRD is the consolidation surface, and its absence is structurally invisible until something forces a single read of "what are we shipping."
